video_title: How to make perfect scented soy wax tin candles every time. DIY tutorial 7oz coco soy wax candles

windows.transcript: These candles have perfect scent throw, great tops, and burn efficiently all the way through, leaving minimal wax behind.
windows.embed_text_v2: finished lit soy candle burning in black metal tin with HYO essence label and fern lid, staged product beauty shot, wax pool, single wick flame, decorative bunnies, potted plant, dim mood lighting
windows.summary_v2: A staged beauty shot of a finished, lit black-tin soy candle (HYO essence) displayed with decorative felt bunnies and a potted plant.

seconds_v2.desc: Dimly lit shot of a finished black tin candle with its lid propped against it on a textured grey surface; felt bunny figurines sit blurred in the background.
Lights up: the lit candle glows warmly, revealing a glossy soy wax pool and a black 'HYO essence' label on the tin, lid resting beside it.
Same staged finished candle, flame glowing inside the tin, fern-print lid leaning at left, two felt bunnies behind.
Scene darkens again; the lit tin candle and its lid remain in place, mostly in shadow.
Closer cool-toned view of the open lit tin candle beside a potted plant, single wood/cotton wick flame burning in the wax pool, fern-print lid in front.
Finished candle continues burning in its black tin, lid showing the 'HYO' fern logo, potted plant at left, bunny at right.
Steady staged shot of the lit finished candle in the tin, small flame at center of the wax surface.
Same finished lit candle composition, flame burning, decorative bunny and plant framing the product.
Camera tilts away into a blurred dark grey blank frame, ending the product beauty shot.

windows.transcript: motion just until you see a thin layer of melted wax forming and try to avoid any direct heat onto the wick itself otherwise it will melt.
windows.embed_text_v2: smoothing tops of five poured soy wax candles with a heat gun, black metal tins in wooden tray with floral liner, upright wicks, melting surface flat and glossy, finishing stage
windows.summary_v2: A heat gun is passed over five freshly poured black-tin soy candles in a tray to melt and smooth their tops.

tags_v2.action: smoothing candle tops with heat gun

seconds_v2.desc: Five black tin candles filled with pale soy wax sit in a wooden serving tray with a gold floral liner; a heat gun nozzle enters at upper right aimed at the rear tins to smooth the tops.
Heat gun lifts away momentarily; the five wax-filled tins show slightly uneven, freshly poured surfaces with upright wicks.
Tins rest in the tray, wax tops still setting, wicks standing; heat gun out of frame.
Heat gun nozzle returns over the center-rear tin, directing hot air to melt and level the top wax layer.
Heat gun hovers over the back-row tins, the warmed wax surface beginning to look glossy and smooth.
Nozzle moves across the middle tin, smoothing the soy wax top with heat.
Heat gun continues over the rear tins, tops becoming flatter and shinier as the wax re-melts.
Heat gun works the center-rear candle, melted wax layer evening out around the wick.
Blue heat gun tilts down toward the front-center tin, hot air rippling its surface to finish smoothing all five candle tops.

windows.embed_text_v2: smoothing five soy wax tin candle tops with blue heat gun, melting surface flat and glossy, black metal tins in wooden floral tray, upright wicks, finishing stage, bare hand adjusting
windows.summary_v2: A heat gun is moved tin-to-tin over five soy candles in a tray, re-melting and smoothing each top to a flat glossy finish.

tags_v2.action: smoothing candle tops with heat gun

seconds_v2.desc: Blue heat gun directs hot air onto the center-rear of five soy-wax tin candles in a wooden floral-lined tray, melting the top to smooth it.
Heat gun nozzle hovers at the back; the five tins show increasingly glossy, leveling wax surfaces with upright wicks.
Nozzle works over the back-left tin, warming the wax until the top flattens and shines.
Heat gun aimed at the front-left tin, its wax surface visibly rippling and re-melting smooth.
Heat gun moves between the front tins, evening out the soy wax tops around the wicks.
Nozzle over the front-left candle ('HEAT GUN' lettering visible on the tool); a bare fingertip appears at top edge adjusting position.
Heat gun lowered into the front-left tin melting its surface; a hand's fingertips briefly visible at the top of frame, no face.
Heat gun pulls up over the rear tins as the smoothed candle tops look uniform and glossy.
Frame darkens; the five finished smoothed candles sit in the tray with the heat gun nozzle just above center.
